NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED (RE: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT) (TRANSACTION ID # 59669653) FILED BY PLAINTIFF DAVIS-HORNBY, SUZANNE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY AND GRANTED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. PLAINTIFF SUZANNE DAVIS-HORNBY PRESENTED EVIDENCE RAISING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THE CITY CREATED A DANGEROUS CONDITION THAT LED TO MS. DAVIS-HORNBY'S INJURIES. FACKRELL V. CITY OF SAN DIEGO (1945) 26 CAL. 2D 196 REMAINS GOOD LAW AND HAS NOT BEEN OVERRULED BY BROWN V. POWAY UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT (1993) 4 CAL.4TH. 820 OR SUPERSEDED BY THE ENACTMENT OF GOVERNMENT CODE 835. BROWN HELD THAT SECTION 835 CODIFIED THE RULE APPLIED IN FACKRELL. (BROWN AT 834.) LIKE THE FACTS IN FACKRELL, MS. DAVIS-HORNBY PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT THE CITY KNEW THE GRANITE DECOMPOSED BACKFILL WOULD ERODE OVER TIME DUE TO ACTS OF NATURE AND OTHER FACTORS AND THE CITY HAD NOTICE THAT OTHER PEOPLE HAD FALLEN IN ERODED TREE BASINS. THEREFORE, SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION ARE DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO RESPOND TO THE CITY'S MOTION AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS A CONCESSION THAT THE CITY IS ENTITLED TO SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THAT CLAIM. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-21-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-11-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Casey Gee of Thompson Law Offices PC (650.513.6111) appears as counsel for plaintiff/non-opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is DENIED as to the first cause of action for dangerous condition of public property and granted as to the second cause of action for negligence. Plaintiff Suzanne Davis-Hornby presented evidence raising a triable issue whether the City created a dangerous condition that led to Ms. Davis-Hornby's injuries. Fackrell v. City of San Diego (1945) 26 Cal. 2d 196 remains good law and has not been overruled by Brown v. Poway Unified School District (1993) 4 Cal.4th. 820 or superseded by the enactment of Government Code 835. Brown held that section 835 codified the rule applied in Fackrell. (Brown at 834.) Like the facts in Fackrell, Ms. Davis-Hornby presents evidence that the City knew the granite decomposed backfill would erode over time due to acts of nature and other factors and the City had notice that other people had fallen in eroded tree basins. Therefore, summary judgment and summary adjudication as to the first cause of action are DENIED. Plaintiff's failure to respond to the City's motion as to the second cause of action is a concession that the City is entitled to summary adjudication on that claim. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
